Fields stability_metric, community_stability, compositional_reproducibility
TARGET Multilab EcoFAB Reproducibility Study (Trent R. Northen)

Multilab study demonstrating highly reproducible plant physiology and soil metabolite depletion patterns using EcoFAB standardized fabricated ecosystems across 4 labs.

Organism: Brachypodium distachyon
Data modalities:
Metabolomics Phenotypic Data
Primary reference: doi:10.1111/nph.15662 - Multilab EcoFAB study shows highly reproducible physiology and depletion of soil metabolites by a model grass
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: EcoFAB-grown Brachypodium plants
Observational units: plant physiology measurements, soil metabolite profiles
Sampling strategy: Standardized EcoFAB experiment repeated across four laboratories to measure plant physiology and soil metabolite depletion.
Replication: Experiment repeated across 4 labs.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.
Study Variables 4
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
laboratory site
Laboratory where the EcoFAB reproducibility experiment was performed.
Blocking Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels four participating laboratories
Entity reproducibility study site
Fields lab, site, laboratory
EcoFAB growth system
Standardized fabricated ecosystem used to grow Brachypodium for reproducibility testing.
Study Design Controlled Variable
Categorical
Levels EcoFAB
Entity plant growth system
Fields growth_system, ecofab_device, device_type
MIxS plant growth medium MIXS:0001057
plant physiology phenotype
Plant physiology measurements used to assess reproducibility across EcoFAB laboratories.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity Brachypodium plant
Method plant physiology phenotyping
Fields plant_physiology, biomass, growth_phenotype
soil metabolite depletion
Soil metabolite depletion profile measured in standardized EcoFAB experiments.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity soil metabolome
Matrix soil
Method metabolomics
Fields soil_metabolite_depletion, metabolite_abundance, depletion_profile
Key Publications 3
doi:10.1126/sciadv.adg7888
  • EcoFAB 2.0 enables highly reproducible plant-microbiome experiments across multiple laboratories
  • Nitrogen form and starvation significantly modulate root exudation patterns
doi:10.1038/s41564-018-0129-3 Dynamic root exudate chemistry and microbial substrate preferences drive patterns in rhizosphere microbial community assembly
  • Root exudate chemistry dynamically changes over plant development
  • Microbial substrate preferences drive rhizosphere community assembly patterns
doi:10.1038/s41467-023-37164-x
  • Identified core metabolome shared across phylogenetically distinct plant species
Technologies Developed 4
EcoFAB
Fabricated ecosystem serving as standardized reproducible platform for plant-microbiome research
ET-Seq
Environmental Transformation Sequencing for assessing DNA uptake in microbial communities
DART
DNA-editing All-in-one RNA-guided CRISPR-Cas Transposase system for locus-specific DNA insertion
EcoPOD
Meter-scale contained controlled ecosystems
Microbes Persist Microbes Persist: Systems Biology of the Soil Microbiome

The Microbes Persist SFA aims to understand how microbes shape soil carbon persistence. Using stable isotope-informed approaches, the team characterizes the "active" microbiome and virome, scaling from metagenomes to computational models integrating traits at system scale.

Lead institution: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
Partners:
University Of California, Berkeley University Of California, Davis University Of Minnesota Northern Arizona University Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Start date: 2017
Established: 2017
Principal investigators:
Jennifer Pett-Ridge
Principal Investigator
Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
Links:
Doe Page Llnl Page Kbase
NMDC umbrella study: nmdc:sty-11-msexsy29
Scientific questions:
  • How do microbial cellular chemistry and ecophysiology shape soil C persistence?
  • What role do viruses play in soil carbon cycling?
  • How do moisture regimes affect microbial transformations?
  • What mechanisms regulate soil organic matter stabilization?
Keywords:
Soil Carbon Soil Microbiome Stable Isotopes Qsip Nanosims Soil Organic Matter Carbon Persistence Virome Moisture Regimes
Data types:
Metagenomics Metatranscriptomics Stable Isotope Data Imaging Data Radiocarbon Data
Modalities:
Stable Isotope Probing Imaging Radioisotope Approaches Multi Omics
Phenotype assays:
Qsip Activity Nanosims Activity Soil Incubation Respiration Growth Curves
Studies 5
nmdc:sty-11-076c9980 - Microbial Carbon Transformations in Wet Tropical Soils (Jennifer Pett-Ridge)

Lab enrichment of tropical soil microbial communities from Luquillo Experimental Forest, Puerto Rico, examining effects of redox fluctuation on microbial ecophysiology.

Source: Microbial Carbon Transformations in Wet Tropical Soils: Effects of Redox Fluctuation
DOE Data Explorer dataset DOI for JGI metagenomic data from redox-fluctuation incubations of Luquillo tropical soils.
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: tropical soil enrichment cultures
Observational units: soil microbial community samples
Sampling strategy: Laboratory enrichment of Luquillo tropical soil microbial communities under redox fluctuation conditions.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description.
Study Variables 19
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
redox fluctuation treatment
Oxic-anoxic or redox fluctuation condition applied to tropical soil enrichments.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity tropical soil enrichment
Fields redox_treatment, redox_fluctuation, perturbation
MIxS perturbation MIXS:0000754
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
tropical soil source

Spatial Index Grouping
Categorical
Entity tropical soil source location
Matrix soil
Fields soil_source, field_site, location
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
soil sampling depth
Soil depth recorded in NMDC biosample metadata for Luquillo tropical soil samples.

Numeric
Units meter
Entity tropical soil sampling interval
Matrix soil
Fields depth, soil_depth
MIxS depth MIXS:0000018
microbial carbon transformation profile

Array
Entity soil carbon transformation process
Matrix tropical soil

Array

TARGET Rhizosphere Microbiome Dynamics with Genome-Informed Energy Budget Model (Jennifer Pett-Ridge)

Predictions of rhizosphere microbiome dynamics using genome-informed and trait-based energy budget model. Published in Nature Microbiology 2024.

Organism: Avena fatua
Data modalities:
Metagenomics Metatranscriptomics
Primary reference: doi:10.1038/s41564-023-01582-w
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Observational units: Avena rhizosphere metagenome samples, Avena rhizosphere metatranscriptome samples, model-predicted microbial populations
Sampling strategy: Genome-informed and trait-based modeling of Avena rhizosphere microbiome dynamics integrated with metagenomic and metatranscriptomic data.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.
Study Variables 4
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
rhizosphere sampling time
Sampling point used to represent temporal dynamics in the Avena rhizosphere microbiome.
Date Time Variable Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity rhizosphere sampling event
Time series yes
Fields time_point, sampling_time, collection_date
MIxS collection date MIXS:0000011
genome-informed trait profile
Genome-derived microbial traits used as inputs to the energy budget model.
Study Design Independent Variable Derived
Array
Entity rhizosphere microbial genome
Method genome annotation and trait inference
Fields genome_trait, trait_profile, energy_budget_trait
predicted energy budget state
Model-predicted microbial energy allocation, growth, or activity state.
Study Design Dependent Variable Derived
Array
Entity rhizosphere microbial population
Method genome-informed trait-based energy budget model
Fields predicted_growth, energy_budget_state, model_prediction
rhizosphere omics profile
Metagenomic and metatranscriptomic profile used to fit or evaluate microbiome dynamics.
Measured Variable Study Design Dependent Variable
Array
Entity Avena rhizosphere microbial community
Matrix rhizosphere
Method metagenomics and metatranscriptomics
Fields metagenome_profile, metatranscriptome_profile, transcript_abundance

TARGET Radiocarbon Analysis of Soil Microbial Biomass (Jennifer Pett-Ridge)

Novel method for radiocarbon analysis of soil microbial biomass via direct chloroform extraction. Enables measurement of microbial C turnover.

Data modalities:
Stable Isotope Data Radiocarbon Data
Primary reference: doi:10.1017/RDC.2023.80 - RADIOCARBON ANALYSIS OF SOIL MICROBIAL BIOMASS VIA DIRECT CHLOROFORM EXTRACTION
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: soil extraction preparations
Observational units: soil microbial biomass carbon fractions
Sampling strategy: Direct chloroform extraction and radiocarbon measurement of soil microbial biomass.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.
Study Variables 4
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
extraction fraction
Soil microbial biomass fraction or extract produced by the direct chloroform extraction method.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Entity soil microbial biomass extract
Matrix soil
Fields extraction_fraction, fraction, chloroform_extract
radiocarbon content
Radiocarbon measurement of soil microbial biomass carbon.
Measured Variable Study Design Dependent Variable
Numeric
Entity microbial biomass carbon
Matrix soil
Method radiocarbon analysis
Fields radiocarbon, delta14C, fraction_modern
microbial biomass carbon
Soil microbial biomass carbon amount measured from the extracted fraction.
Measured Variable Study Design Dependent Variable
Numeric
Entity soil microbial biomass
Matrix soil
Method direct chloroform extraction
Fields microbial_biomass_carbon, biomass_C, extracted_carbon
Notes BERVO has a generic microbial biomass chemical element term but no carbon-specific microbial biomass term.
BERVO microbial biomass chemical element BERVO:0000995
microbial carbon turnover estimate
Inferred microbial carbon turnover enabled by radiocarbon analysis of microbial biomass.
Derived Study Design Dependent Variable
Numeric
Entity soil microbial carbon turnover
Method radiocarbon-based turnover inference
Fields turnover_estimate, carbon_turnover, microbial_C_turnover
TARGET MISIP Data Standard for Stable Isotope Probing (Jennifer Pett-Ridge)

Data standard for reuse and reproducibility of stable isotope probing-derived nucleic acid sequences. Published in GigaScience 2024.

Primary reference: doi:10.1093/gigascience/giae071 - MISIP: a data standard for the reuse and reproducibility of any stable isotope probing-derived nucleic acid sequence and experiment
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Observational units: stable isotope probing experiment metadata records, SIP-derived nucleic acid sequence records
Sampling strategy: Data-standard specification of reusable metadata fields for stable isotope probing experiments and derived nucleic acid sequences.
Notes: Variable catalog entry treats MISIP-required metadata fields as data dictionary variables.
Study Variables 4
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
isotope label or substrate
Stable isotope label and labeled substrate used in a stable isotope probing experiment.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity SIP labeling treatment
Fields isotope_label, labeled_substrate, substrate_label
MIxS chemical administration MIXS:0000751
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
nucleic acid fraction
Density fraction or nucleic acid fraction from which SIP-derived sequence data were generated.
Grouping Identifier
Identifier
Entity SIP nucleic acid fraction
Fields fraction_id, gradient_fraction, nucleic_acid_fraction
buoyant density
Buoyant density associated with a SIP gradient fraction.
Measured Variable Covariate
Numeric
Units gram per milliliter
Entity SIP gradient fraction
Fields buoyant_density, density, fraction_density
SIP sequence accession
Identifier linking MISIP metadata to SIP-derived nucleic acid sequence data.
Identifier
Identifier
Entity nucleic acid sequence record
Fields sequence_accession, run_accession, biosample_accession
HIGH California Grassland qSIP Metagenomes (Jennifer Pett-Ridge)

Quantitative stable isotope probing with metagenomics linking microbial physiology and activity to soil moisture in Mediterranean-climate grassland ecosystems.

Organism: soil metagenome
Data modalities:
Metagenomics Stable Isotope Data
Samples: 332
BioProjects: PRJNA718849
Primary reference: doi:10.1128/msystems.00417-22
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Observational units: grassland soil metagenome samples
Sampling strategy: Quantitative stable isotope probing across soil moisture conditions.
Replication: 332 metagenomic samples.
Notes: Initial variable catalog entry based on study description.
Study Variables 3
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
soil moisture
Soil water availability condition associated with qSIP metagenome samples.
Study Design Independent Variable Covariate
Numeric
Units gram per gram or cubic centimeter per cubic centimeter
Entity soil water content
Matrix soil
Fields soil_moisture, water_content, volumetric_water_content
MIxS water content MIXS:0000185
stable isotope amendment
Isotopically labeled substrate or amendment used for quantitative stable isotope probing.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Method quantitative stable isotope probing
Fields isotope_label, isotope_amendment, substrate_label
MIxS chemical administration MIXS:0000751
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
qSIP-derived microbial activity
Genome-resolved microbial activity inferred from isotope incorporation in metagenomic data.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable Derived
Array
Entity soil microbial populations
Matrix soil
Method quantitative stable isotope probing with metagenomics
Fields qsip_activity, isotope_incorporation, genome_activity_estimate
Key Publications 3
doi:10.1128/msystems.00417-22
  • First application of qSIP to all organisms in soil simultaneously
  • Bacterial growth occurred rapidly after wet-up with ~40% of taxa growing within one week
doi:10.1038/s41564-023-01582-w
  • Genome-informed trait-based models can predict rhizosphere microbiome dynamics
doi:10.1073/pnas.2413032122 Codon bias, nucleotide selection, and genome size predict in situ bacterial growth rate and transcription in rewetted soil
  • Genomic traits including codon bias predict bacterial growth rates in soil
Datasets 1
Microbes Persist qSIP Metagenome Bins

Microbial genome bins and annotations from qSIP metagenomics

Repository: ggKbase
URL: https://ggkbase.berkeley.edu/wsip-metawrap-drep-bins/organisms
Soil Microbiome SFA Phenotypic Response of the Soil Microbiome to Environmental Perturbations

PNNL's Soil Microbiome SFA focuses on understanding how interactions among soil microbial community members lead to emergence of community functions. Research identifies molecular-level metabolic interactions governing decomposition and moisture effects on microbial phenotypes.

Lead institution: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Principal investigators:
Kirsten Hofmockel
Principal Investigator
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Links:
Main Doe Page Data Hub Kbase
Scientific questions:
  • How do microbial interactions lead to emergent community functions?
  • What predictable reaction modules exist based on resource availability?
  • How does soil desiccation affect metabolic processes?
  • What controls carbon use efficiency in soils?
Keywords:
Soil Microbiome Metaphenome Decomposition Moisture Effects Carbon Use Efficiency Microbial Interactions Tall Wheatgrass
Data types:
Metagenomics Metabolomics Imaging Data Imaging Data
Modalities:
Imaging Metagenomics Laboratory Experiments
Phenotype assays:
Soil Incubation Respiration Exometabolomics Substrate Utilization
Field Sites 1
Tall Wheatgrass Irrigation Trial

Testing discoveries in bioenergy feedstock system

Programs: Phenotypic Response of the Soil Microbiome to Environmental Perturbations
Studies 4
TARGET Hi-C Metagenome Sequencing of Soil Moisture Impacts on Phage-Host Interactions (Kirsten Hofmockel)

High-throughput chromosomal conformation capture (Hi-C) metagenome sequencing revealing soil moisture impacts on phage-host interactions. Published in Nature Comm 2023.

Data modalities:
Metagenomics
Primary reference: doi:10.1038/s41467-023-42967-z
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: soil microcosms or field soil samples
Observational units: Hi-C metagenome samples, phage-host linkage records
Sampling strategy: Hi-C metagenome sequencing to identify phage-host interactions across soil moisture conditions.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.
Study Variables 4
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
soil moisture condition
Soil water availability condition used to assess moisture impacts on phage-host interactions.
Study Design Independent Variable Covariate
Numeric
Entity soil water content
Matrix soil
Fields soil_moisture, water_content, moisture_condition
BERVO total soil water content BERVO:0000301
MIxS water content MIXS:0000185
MIxS watering regimen MIXS:0000591
Hi-C contact signal
Chromosomal conformation capture contact evidence used to link viral and host sequences.
Measured Variable Derived
Array
Entity viral-host DNA contact
Matrix soil metagenome
Method Hi-C metagenome sequencing
Fields hic_contact, contact_matrix, read_pair_link
phage-host interaction
Inferred interaction or linkage between phage sequence and microbial host genome.
Study Design Dependent Variable Derived
Array
Entity soil phage-host pair
Method Hi-C link inference from metagenomic data
Fields phage_host_link, virus_host_pair, host_prediction
viral contig or MAG
Viral contig, viral genome, or microbial genome bin used in phage-host interaction analysis.
Identifier Derived
Identifier
Entity viral or microbial genome sequence
Method metagenome assembly and binning
Fields viral_contig_id, mag_id, genome_id
TARGET Soil Microbiome Response to Depth, Irrigation and Plant Genotype (Kirsten Hofmockel)

Influence of soil depth, irrigation, and plant genotype on the soil microbiome, metaphenome, and carbon chemistry. Multi-omics study published in mBio 2023.

Data modalities:
Metagenomics Metabolomics Proteomics
Primary reference: doi:10.1128/mbio.01758-23
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: planted soil microcosms
Observational units: soil multi-omics samples
Sampling strategy: Multi-omics profiling across soil depth, irrigation, and plant genotype conditions.
Notes: Initial variable catalog entry based on study description.
Study Variables 5
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
soil depth
Sampling depth below the soil surface.
Study Design Independent Variable Spatial Index
Numeric
Units centimeter
Entity soil profile
Matrix soil
Fields depth, soil_depth
MIxS depth MIXS:0000018
irrigation treatment
Watering or irrigation condition applied to the soil and plant system.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity soil-plant system
Fields irrigation, irrigation_treatment, watering_regimen
MIxS watering regimen MIXS:0000591
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
plant genotype
Host plant genotype associated with each soil multi-omics sample.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Entity host plant
Fields plant_genotype, host_genotype
MIxS host genotype MIXS:0000365
soil microbiome composition
Metagenome-derived microbial community composition across depth, irrigation, and genotype treatments.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity soil microbial community
Matrix soil
Method metagenomics
Fields metagenome_profile, taxonomic_profile, functional_profile
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
metabolite and protein feature abundances
Metabolomic and proteomic feature abundances measured from the same soil system.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity soil metaphenome
Matrix soil
Method metabolomics and proteomics
Fields metabolite_abundance, protein_abundance, metaphenome_features
TARGET Birch Effect Respiratory Response of Soil Microbiome (Kirsten Hofmockel)

Real-time and rapid respiratory response (90 seconds to 90 minutes) of the soil microbiome to moisture shifts. Published in Microorganisms 2023.

Data modalities:
Metabolomics
Primary reference: doi:10.3390/microorganisms11112630 - Real-Time and Rapid Respiratory Response of the Soil Microbiome to Moisture Shifts
Study Design
Design type: time series design OBI:0500020
Experimental units: rewetted soil incubations
Observational units: real-time respiration measurements, soil metabolite measurements
Sampling strategy: Rapid time-series measurement of soil microbiome respiration from 90 seconds to 90 minutes after moisture shifts.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.
Study Variables 4
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
moisture shift treatment
Drying or rewetting moisture perturbation applied to soil microbiome incubations.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity soil moisture perturbation
Matrix soil
Fields moisture_shift, rewetting_treatment, drying_rewetting
BERVO total soil water content BERVO:0000301
MIxS perturbation MIXS:0000754
MIxS watering regimen MIXS:0000591
response elapsed time
Time after moisture shift at which respiration was measured.
Date Time Variable Study Design Independent Variable
Numeric
Units seconds or minutes
Entity respiration sampling event
Time series yes; 90 seconds to 90 minutes
Fields elapsed_time, time_after_rewetting, response_time
soil respiration rate
Rapid respiratory response of the soil microbiome after moisture shift.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity soil microbial respiration
Matrix soil
Method real-time respiration measurement
Fields respiration_rate, CO2_flux, respiratory_response
metabolite response profile
Metabolomic response associated with rapid soil microbiome remodeling after moisture shift.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity soil metabolome
Matrix soil
Method metabolomics
Fields metabolite_abundance, metabolite_response, feature_table
TARGET Viral Contributions to Soil Carbon Dynamics (Kirsten Hofmockel)

Estimating the importance of viral contributions to soil carbon dynamics through metagenomics. Published in Global Change Biology 2024.

Data modalities:
Metagenomics
Primary reference: doi:10.1111/gcb.17524 - Estimating the Importance of Viral Contributions to Soil Carbon Dynamics
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Observational units: soil metagenome samples, soil viral genome records
Sampling strategy: Metagenomic estimation of soil viral diversity, auxiliary metabolic potential, and contributions to carbon dynamics.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.

Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
soil virus abundance or diversity
Soil virome abundance, richness, or diversity inferred from metagenomic data.
Measured Variable Study Design Dependent Variable
Array
Entity soil viral community
Matrix soil
Method metagenomics
Fields viral_abundance, viral_diversity, virome_profile
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
viral auxiliary metabolic gene profile
Viral genes or functions with potential roles in soil carbon cycling.
Measured Variable Derived
Array
Entity soil viral genome
Method viral genome annotation
Fields auxiliary_metabolic_gene, AMG_profile, viral_function
soil carbon dynamics estimate
Estimated contribution of viruses to soil carbon pools, fluxes, or transformations.
Study Design Dependent Variable Derived
Array
Entity soil carbon dynamics
Matrix soil
Method metagenomics-informed carbon dynamics estimation
Fields viral_carbon_contribution, carbon_dynamics_estimate, carbon_flux
soil sample context
Soil site, matrix, or environmental context for viral carbon dynamics estimates.
Spatial Index Grouping
Categorical
Entity soil sampling context
Matrix soil
Fields site, soil_context, environmental_medium
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
Key Publications 5
doi:10.1038/s41467-023-42967-z
  • High-throughput chromosomal conformation capture reveals soil moisture controls phage-host dynamics
doi:10.1128/mbio.01758-23
  • Soil depth and irrigation interact to shape microbiome composition and metabolic phenotypes
doi:10.1038/s41564-024-01686-x A global atlas of soil viruses reveals unexplored biodiversity and potential biogeochemical impacts
  • Global soil virome atlas reveals vast unexplored viral diversity with biogeochemical implications
doi:10.3390/microorganisms11112630 Real-time and rapid respiratory response of the soil microbiome to moisture shifts
  • Soil microbiome responds to moisture within 90 seconds to 90 minutes (Birch effect)
doi:10.1186/s40168-022-01427-4
  • Extreme weather events cause significant restructuring of soil lipid chemistry

IMAGINE IMAGINE BioSecurity: Integrative Modeling and Genome-Scale Engineering for Biosystems Security

IMAGINE develops an integrative biocontainment platform to build, characterize, and predictively design biocontainment in microbial systems. The SFA integrates synthetic biology, computational modeling, and plant-soil microbiology for secure biosystems design.

Lead institution: National Renewable Energy Laboratory
Partners:
J. Craig Venter Institute University Of California, San Diego
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Links:
Doe Page
Scientific questions:
  • How do we achieve predictive control of engineered biosystems?
  • What biocontainment strategies maintain fitness and productivity?
  • How can genome-scale engineering enable secure deployment?
  • What tracking mechanisms ensure biosecurity?
Keywords:
Biocontainment Biosecurity Synthetic Biology Genome Engineering Microbial Chassis Combigem Dna Barcoding
Data types:
Genomics Fitness Data Containment Efficacy Data
Modalities:
High Throughput Screening Genome Scale Engineering High Throughput Sequencing
Key Publications 4
doi:10.1016/j.copbio.2021.05.004 Biotechnology for secure biocontainment designs in an emerging bioeconomy
  • Review of biocontainment strategies for safe deployment of engineered microbes
doi:10.1021/acssynbio.3c00183 Improved Combinatorial Assembly and Barcode Sequencing for Gene-Sized DNA Constructs
  • Improved methods for combinatorial DNA assembly enabling rapid biocontainment testing
doi:10.3389/fpls.2022.839446 Biocontainment of Genetically Engineered Algae
  • Biocontainment strategies developed for algal biofuel production systems
doi:10.1007/s00248-023-02321-8 Risk Assessment of Industrial Microbes Using a Terrestrial Mesocosm Platform
  • Mesocosm platform enables environmental risk assessment of engineered microbes
BFI Bacterial-Fungal Interactions and Their Role in Soil Functioning

The BFI SFA discovers fundamental principles underlying bacterial-fungal interactions to harness their biotechnological potential for steering soil ecosystem functions. Research uses novel bioinformatics and multiomics approaches to understand how bacteria and fungi sense, respond to, and co-evolve with one another.

Lead institution: Los Alamos National Laboratory
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Principal investigators:
Patrick Chain
Principal Investigator
Los Alamos National Laboratory
Links:
Doe Page Kbase Bfi Portal
Scientific questions:
  • What principles drive bacterial-fungal interactions in soil?
  • How do BFI affect carbon flux and nutrient cycling?
  • How can we steer ecosystem functions through BFI manipulation?
  • What molecular mechanisms mediate BFI?
Keywords:
Bacterial Fungal Interactions Endohyphal Bacteria Fungal Highways Siderophores Soil Microbiome Carbon Flux Nutrient Cycling Plant Productivity Co Evolution
Data types:
Metagenomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics Amplicon 16s Amplicon Its Imaging Data Fish Imaging Siderophore Data Endohyphal Microbiome Data Bacterial Fungal Interaction Data Fungal Highway Data
Modalities:
Multi Omics Imaging Computational Modeling Targeted Metagenomics Fungal Highway Columns Fish Microscopy Coculture Experiments Sequence Enrichment
Phenotype assays:
Bacterial Fungal Interaction Assay Fungal Highway Assay Endohyphal Screening Internalization Assay Siderophore Assay Coculture Interactions Exometabolomics Live Cell Imaging Growth Curves Competition Assays
Field Sites 3
Valles Caldera National Preserve (Jemez Mountains, New Mexico)

Montane grassland site at highest elevation in the BFI gradient. Part of elevation/precipitation transect studying bacterial-fungal interactions across environmental conditions.

Type: National Preserve
Elevation (m): 2466
Mean annual temp (C): 6
Mean annual precip (cm): 59
Programs: Bacterial-Fungal Interactions and Their Role in Soil Functioning
Los Alamos National Laboratory (Los Alamos, New Mexico)

Mid-elevation piñon-juniper woodland site. Intermediate conditions in the BFI elevation/precipitation gradient for studying bacterial-fungal interactions.

Type: National Laboratory
Elevation (m): 2231
Mean annual temp (C): 12
Mean annual precip (cm): 30
Programs: Bacterial-Fungal Interactions and Their Role in Soil Functioning
Sevilleta LTER (Sevilleta National Wildlife Refuge, New Mexico)

Desert grassland site at lowest elevation in the BFI gradient. Represents marginal lands with arid conditions for studying bacterial-fungal interactions under water limitation.

Type: Lter
Elevation (m): 1511
Mean annual temp (C): 14
Mean annual precip (cm): 2
Programs: Bacterial-Fungal Interactions and Their Role in Soil Functioning
Isolate Collections 2
BFI Bacterial Collection

Bacterial isolates used for bacterial-fungal interaction studies. Includes strains selected for their ability to interact with diverse fungal partners.

Organism types:
Bacteria
Source environments:
Soil Rhizosphere
Isolation methods: Aerobic plating
BFI Fungal Collection

Fungal isolates including soil fungi and model organisms used for bacterial-fungal interaction studies.

Organism types:
Fungi
Source environments:
Soil Rhizosphere
Isolation methods: Fungal plating, Hyphal tip isolation
Key Publications 3
doi:10.1016/j.funbio.2023.04.002 A centralized resource for bacterial-fungal interactions research
  • BFI Research Portal provides centralized database of reported bacterial-fungal interactions
  • Resource serves as foundation for computational predictions of novel interactions
doi:10.1038/s41467-024-48812-1 Enhanced surface colonisation and competition during bacterial adaptation to a fungus
  • Bacteria adapt to fungal presence through enhanced surface colonization strategies
doi:10.1039/d3an00408b Metabolomics of bacterial-fungal pairwise interactions reveal conserved molecular mechanisms
  • Conserved metabolic mechanisms underlie diverse bacterial-fungal interactions
Datasets 4
BFI Research Portal Database

Curated database of literature-derived bacterial-fungal interaction records. Users can query bacterial or fungal taxa to identify known interaction partners. Includes interactive Krona visualizations and supports user submission of new interaction reports.

URL: https://sfa-bfi.edgebioinformatics.org/search
Fungal Highway Column Dataset

Data from 3D-printed fungal highway column experiments studying bacterial dispersal via fungal mycelia. Includes 16S/ITS amplicon data and metagenomes (150-250 bp paired-end, 10-20 Gb/sample) from soil, dung, and rhizosphere substrates.

Endohyphal Microbiome Database

Genomic and imaging data on microorganisms living within fungal hyphae, including Mollicutes-related endobacteria (MRE), viruses, cyanobacteria, algae, archaea, and plastids detected in fungal cells.

BFI Metabolomics Interaction Data

Exometabolomics data from bacterial-fungal co-cultures including siderophore profiles (ferrichrome, coprogen, novel analogues), iron/biotin availability measurements, and metabolite exchange dynamics.

Biofuels SFA Solvent Disruption of Biomass and Biomembranes

ORNL Biofuels SFA studying how amphiphilic solvents alter plant cell-wall and microbial membrane assemblies during biomass pretreatment and microbial fermentation. The program combines neutron scattering, NMR spectroscopy, molecular dynamics, biodeuteration, bacterial membrane engineering, and plant cell-wall chemistry to improve production of biofuels and bioproducts.

Lead institution: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Partners:
University Of Cincinnati University Of Tennessee University Of California, Riverside
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Principal investigators:
Brian Davison
Principal Investigator
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Co-investigators:
Barbara R. Evans
Technical Co-Manager
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
James Elkins
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
John Katsaras
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Jonathan Nickels
University of Cincinnati
Hugh M. O'Neill
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Arthur J. Ragauskas
University of Tennessee
Loukas Petridis
University of Tennessee
Sai Venkatesh Pingali
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Jeremy C. Smith
University of Tennessee
Links:
Main Doe Page Publications
Scientific questions:
  • How do amphiphilic solvents disrupt plant biomass and microbial biomembranes?
  • Which solvent properties are best suited for biomass disruption and fractionation?
  • What are the biological limits of microbial tolerance to high solvent levels?
  • How can predictive models and biophysical characterization guide pretreatment and fermentation design?
Keywords:
Bioenergy Biofuels Bioproducts Biomass Recalcitrance Plant Cell Walls Lignocellulose Microbial Conversion Ionic Liquids
Data types:
Structural Biology Phenotypic Data Imaging Data Enzyme Activity Data Synthetic Biology Constructs
Modalities:
Nmr Spectroscopy Synchrotron Methods Imaging Computational Modeling Laboratory Experiments Fermentation Studies
Studies 1
Solvent Disruption of Biomass and Biomembrane Structures (Brian Davison)

Integrated experimental and computational study of amphiphilic co-solvent effects on lignocellulosic biomass, plant cell-wall polymers, and microbial membrane lipid bilayers.

Organism: bioenergy feedstocks and solvent-tolerant microbes
Data modalities:
Structural Biology Phenotypic Data Imaging Data
Primary reference: doi:10.1039/D4GC00510D - Multiscale Investigation of the Mechanism of Biomass Deconstruction in the Dimethyl Isosorbide/Water Co-Solvent Pretreatment System
Source: Solvent Disruption of Biomass and Biomembranes
DOE Genomic Science program page describing the ORNL Biomass Deconstruction SFA.
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: solvent-treated biomass samples, solvent-exposed microbial membrane systems, molecular dynamics simulations
Observational units: plant cell-wall structural measurements, membrane structural measurements, pretreatment and fermentation performance measurements
Sampling strategy: Laboratory and simulation-based comparison of solvent classes, solvent concentrations, biomass substrates, and biomembrane systems.
Controls: Untreated biomass or membrane systems and solvent-free baseline simulations.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on DOE and ORNL SFA descriptions.
Study Variables 6
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
amphiphilic solvent identity
Solvent or co-solvent used to disrupt biomass or biomembrane assemblies.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Levels ethanol, butanol, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl isosorbide, deep eutectic solvent
Entity solvent treatment
Fields solvent, cosolvent, solvent_identity
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
solvent concentration
Concentration or fraction of solvent used in pretreatment, membrane exposure, or simulation.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Numeric
Entity solvent treatment
Fields solvent_concentration, cosolvent_fraction, solvent_loading
biomass or biomembrane substrate
Plant biomass, cell-wall polymer, or microbial membrane system exposed to solvent.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels cellulose, lignin, switchgrass biomass, microbial lipid bilayer
Entity biomass or biomembrane substrate
Fields substrate, biomass_type, membrane_system
biomolecular assembly structure
Structural state of biomass polymers or lipid bilayers measured after solvent exposure.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity plant cell-wall or biomembrane assembly
Method neutron scattering, NMR spectroscopy, microscopy, or molecular dynamics
Fields scattering_profile, nmr_spectrum, membrane_order_parameter, cellulose_fibril_spacing
biomass deconstruction performance
Pretreatment effectiveness measured as biomass disruption, fractionation, or sugar release.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity lignocellulosic biomass
Fields deconstruction_yield, sugar_release, lignin_solubilization, fractionation_efficiency
microbial solvent tolerance
Microbial growth, membrane stability, or viability under solvent exposure.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity fermenting microbial cell
Matrix microbial culture
Fields growth_rate, viability, membrane_integrity, solvent_tolerance
Key Publications 2
doi:10.1039/D4GC00510D Multiscale Investigation of the Mechanism of Biomass Deconstruction in the Dimethyl Isosorbide/Water Co-Solvent Pretreatment System
  • Multiscale measurements and simulations can connect co-solvent pretreatment chemistry to biomass deconstruction mechanisms.
doi:10.1021/acssuschemeng.2c06876 Amphiphilic co-solvents modulate structure of membrane domains
  • Amphiphilic co-solvents alter membrane-domain structure relevant to microbial solvent tolerance.
Datasets 2
ORNL Biofuels SFA Publications

Publication list for the ORNL Biomass Deconstruction SFA, including solvent, biomass, membrane, scattering, and molecular simulation studies.

Repository: ORNL Biofuels SFA
URL: https://sfa-biofuels.ornl.gov/publications/
Data type: publication index
Protein Co-Evolutionary Analysis and Virtual Ligand Screening

KBase collaboration associated with the ORNL Biofuels SFA.

Repository: KBase
URL: https://www.kbase.us/
Data type: computational analysis narrative
Persistence Control Persistence Control of Engineered Functions in Complex Soil Microbiomes

PNNL SFA developing principles and tools for persistence control of engineered microbial functions in sorghum rhizosphere microbiomes. The program uses genome editing, high-throughput functional genomics, synthetic communities, laboratory and field rhizosphere experiments, and predictive modeling to increase persistence near target plants while reducing persistence in bulk soil or plant-detritus environments.

Lead institution: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Partners:
Stanford University Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory Oak Ridge National Laboratory University Of Washington University Of California, Berkeley University Of Arizona University Of California, Santa Barbara
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Principal investigators:
Robert Egbert
Principal Investigator
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Co-investigators:
Vanessa Bailey
Laboratory Research Manager
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Joshua Elmore
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Pubudu Handakumbura
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Jason McDermott
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Young-Mo Kim
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory
Jennifer Brophy
Stanford University
Adam Deutschbauer
Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
Adam Guss
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Carrie Harwood
University of Washington
Devin Coleman-Derr
University of California, Berkeley
Links:
Doe Page Data Page
Scientific questions:
  • Which genes and catabolic pathways determine rhizosphere persistence of engineered microbes?
  • How can genome reduction and synthetic metabolic addictions restrict microbial niches?
  • How do synthetic communities and complex rhizosphere microbiomes affect persistence control escape?
  • Can computational models predict persistence phenotypes from genotype and environmental context?
Keywords:
Rhizosphere Sorghum Plant Growth Promoting Bacteria Biocontainment Biosystems Design Genome Engineering Microbial Invasion Soil Microbiome
Data types:
Genomics Metagenomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics Fitness Data Synthetic Biology Constructs
Modalities:
Genome Scale Engineering Crispr Gene Editing High Throughput Screening High Throughput Sequencing Multi Omics Fabricated Ecosystems Field Experiments Computational Modeling
Studies 1
MEDIUM Persistence Control Engineering in Sorghum Rhizosphere Bacteria (Robert Egbert)

Genome editing and phenotype modeling of non-model sorghum rhizosphere bacteria to promote persistence near sorghum roots while reducing persistence in bulk soil or scavenging environments.

Organism: sorghum rhizosphere bacteria
Data modalities:
Genomics Metagenomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics Fitness Data
Source: Persistence Control of Engineered Functions in Complex Soil Microbiomes
DOE Genomic Science program page describing the PNNL Persistence Control SFA.
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: genome-edited non-model rhizosphere bacterial strains, synthetic rhizosphere communities, sorghum rhizosphere microcosms and field plots
Observational units: microbial persistence and fitness measurements, edited-gene and pathway records, community-composition profiles
Sampling strategy: Laboratory-to-field testing of engineered microbial persistence across increasing rhizosphere community complexity and plant-associated environmental contexts.
Controls: Wild-type strains, unedited strains, bulk-soil contexts, and plant-free scavenging environments.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on DOE and EMSL Persistence Control project descriptions.
Study Variables 6
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
engineered rhizosphere bacterial strain
Non-model rhizosphere bacterial strain selected or engineered for persistence control.
Identifier Study Design Independent Variable
Identifier
Entity engineered rhizosphere bacterium
Fields strain_id, isolate_id, engineered_strain
genome edit or pathway perturbation
Genome reduction, catabolic pathway addition/removal, or synthetic metabolic addiction used to alter environmental niche.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity engineered microbial genotype
Fields genome_edit, pathway_perturbation, catabolic_pathway
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
rhizosphere environmental context
Plant-associated, bulk-soil, detritus, or necromass environment used to test persistence.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels sorghum rhizosphere, bulk soil, plant detritus, microbial necromass
Entity microbial niche context
Matrix soil or rhizosphere
Fields environment, niche, env_local_scale
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
Map rhizosphere ENVO:00005801
sorghum root exudate availability
Presence or concentration of sorghum-derived root exudates used to create synthetic metabolic dependency.
Experimental Factor Covariate
Numeric
Entity sorghum root exudate pool
Matrix rhizosphere
Fields root_exudate, exudate_concentration, plant_specific_metabolite
microbial persistence phenotype
Persistence, colonization, abundance, or fitness of engineered bacteria under tested community and environmental contexts.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity engineered rhizosphere bacterial population
Method fitness assay, abundance tracking, sequencing, or culture-based enumeration
Fields persistence, colonization, relative_abundance, fitness_score
persistence control escape
Evidence of escape from the intended persistence-control design through environmental complementation or community interactions.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable Derived
Numeric
Entity engineered microbial containment outcome
Fields escape_frequency, complementation_escape, persistence_escape_score

Repository: EMSL
URL: https://www.emsl.pnnl.gov/project/60649
DOI: doi:10.46936/cpcy.proj.2022.60649/60008751

Repository: EMSL
URL: https://search.emsl.pnnl.gov/?project_id_search=60649
Data type: EMSL project data
PMI Plant-Microbe Interfaces

PMI reveals mechanistic bases underpinning selection of symbiotic plant-microbe partnerships and how environment structures the host plant's microbiome. Various Populus species (eastern cottonwood, black cottonwood, aspen) serve as model hosts for bioenergy research.

Lead institution: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Start date: 2008
Established: 2008
Principal investigators:
Mitchel Doktycz
Principal Investigator
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Dale Pelletier
Bacterial Collection Lead
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Melissa Cregger
Microbiome Ecology Lead
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Links:
Main Ornl Page Doe Page Kbase
NMDC umbrella study: nmdc:sty-11-f1he1955
KBase Collection: PMI
Scientific questions:
  • How do plants select symbiotic microbial partners?
  • What environmental factors structure the plant microbiome?
  • How do microbial community composition and host genetics interact?
  • What metabolites mediate plant-microbe communication?
Keywords:
Plant Microbe Interactions Populus Poplar Rhizosphere Endophytes Mycorrhizae Bioenergy Crops
Data types:
Genomics Metagenomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics Isolate Collections
Modalities:
Bacterial Isolation Genome Sequencing Multi Omics Imaging
Phenotype assays:
Growth Curves Biolog Phenotyping Plant Colonization Coculture Interactions Synthetic Community Exometabolomics Live Cell Imaging
Isolate Collections 2
PMI Bacterial Collection 3500 isolates

Bacterial isolates from native, field, and greenhouse-grown Populus trees. Includes rhizosphere, endosphere, and phyllosphere isolates characterized by 16S rDNA sequencing with full genome sequences for many strains. Represents ~50% of the natural community based on phylogenetic analyses, with multiple representatives of all highly abundant genera.

Organism types:
Bacteria
Genomes sequenced: 750
Source environments:
Populus Rhizosphere Populus Root Endosphere Populus Phyllosphere
Host organisms: Populus trichocarpa, Populus deltoides
Isolation methods: Aerobic plating, Root homogenization
Culture collection: Browse/Request
Genome catalog: View genomes
Reference: doi:10.1128/mSystems.01306-20 - Cultivating the Bacterial Microbiota of <i>Populus</i> Roots
PMI Fungal Collection 3000 isolates

Fungal isolates representing diverse guilds including ectomycorrhizal (ECM), endophytic (END), pathogens, and saprotrophs. Identified key symbiotic taxa and established new models for plant-fungal symbiosis research.

Organism types:
Fungi
Genomes sequenced: 100
Source environments:
Populus Rhizosphere Populus Root Endosphere Forest Soil
Host organisms: Populus trichocarpa, Populus deltoides
Studies 7
nmdc:sty-11-1t150432 - Defining the functional diversity of the Populus root microbiome (Mitchel J. Doktycz)

Characterization and interpretation of plant-microbe interfaces using poplar and its microbial community in the context of favorable plant microbe interactions.

Primary reference: doi:10.1128/mSystems.00045-18
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Observational units: Populus root microbiome samples, Populus-associated microbial isolate genomes
Sampling strategy: Characterization of poplar root microbiome membership and functional diversity in the context of favorable plant-microbe interactions.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.

HIGH nmdc:sty-11-r2h77870 - Bio-Scales - Integrated Metagenomic, Metabolomic and Transcriptomic Survey of Populus (Mitchel J. Doktycz)

Bio-Scales pilot project (PIs: Doktycz, Schadt, Mayes, Muchero, Weston) understanding how plant traits modify the microbiome and how the coupled plant-soil-microbial system influences nitrogen transformation. Integrated dataset of 318 metagenomes, 98 transcriptomes, and 314 metabolomic profiles from 27 P. trichocarpa genotypes across Oregon field sites. Largest publicly available metagenomics repository on tree endosphere.

Organism: Populus trichocarpa
Data modalities:
Metagenomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics
Samples: 318
BioProjects: PRJNA1034652
Primary reference: doi:10.1038/s41597-024-03069-7
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: Populus genotype
Observational units: plant compartment microbiome samples
Sampling strategy: Integrated sampling across Populus genotypes, plant compartments, and Oregon field sites.
Replication: 318 metagenomes, 98 transcriptomes, and 314 metabolomic profiles from 27 genotypes.
Notes: Initial high-level design summary for variable catalog display.
Study Variables 35
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
Populus genotype
Host plant genotype associated with each multi-omics sample.

Categorical
Levels 27 P. trichocarpa genotypes
Entity Populus trichocarpa
Fields genotype, host_genotype

plant compartment
Plant-associated compartment sampled for microbiome and metabolomic profiling.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels root endosphere, rhizosphere, soil
Entity Populus-associated microbial habitat
Fields compartment, sample_type

MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014

HIGH Seasonality and Temporal Dynamics in Populus Microbiome (Melissa Cregger)

Amplicon sequencing characterizing bacterial/archaeal and fungal communities in leaf endosphere, root endosphere, and rhizosphere of two Populus species over time.

Organism: Populus trichocarpa
Data modalities:
Amplicon 16s Amplicon Its
Samples: 2359
BioProjects: PRJNA993999
Primary reference: doi:10.1128/msystems.00886-23
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: clonally propagated Populus plants
Observational units: leaf endosphere samples, root endosphere samples, rhizosphere samples
Sampling strategy: Common-garden sampling of two Populus species over four seasons across two consecutive years.
Replication: Multiple genotypes and replicate plants sampled across time points and plant-associated habitats.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on the primary publication and study description.
Study Variables 5
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
sampling season
Seasonal sampling interval used to test temporal dynamics in the Populus microbiome.
Date Time Variable Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Entity sampling event
Time series yes; four seasons across two consecutive years
Fields season, collection_date, sampling_date
MIxS season MIXS:0000829
MIxS collection date MIXS:0000011
sampling year
Year of repeated Populus microbiome sampling used to separate seasonal and longer-term dynamics.
Date Time Variable Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Integer
Entity sampling event
Time series yes
Fields year, sampling_year, collection_date
MIxS collection date MIXS:0000011
Populus species
Host Populus species included in the common-garden temporal microbiome study.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels Populus deltoides, Populus trichocarpa
Entity Populus host plant
Fields species, host_species, host_common_name
MIxS host common name MIXS:0000248
plant-associated habitat
Populus habitat sampled for bacterial, archaeal, and fungal microbiome profiling.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels leaf endosphere, root endosphere, rhizosphere
Entity Populus-associated microbial habitat
Fields habitat, compartment, sample_type
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
Map rhizosphere ENVO:00005801
bacterial archaeal and fungal community composition
Amplicon-derived community profiles for Populus bacterial, archaeal, and fungal communities.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity Populus-associated microbial community
Matrix leaf endosphere, root endosphere, and rhizosphere
Method 16S rRNA and ITS amplicon sequencing
Fields bacterial_archaeal_profile, fungal_community_profile, asv_table, otu_table
MEDIUM Populus Holobiont Niche and Genotype Effects (Melissa Cregger)

Dissecting effects of plant niches and genotype on the Populus microbiome using 16S and ITS amplicon sequencing across multiple tissue types.

Organism: Populus deltoides
Data modalities:
Amplicon 16s Amplicon Its
Samples: 590
BioProjects: PRJNA385484, PRJNA384978
Primary reference: doi:10.1186/s40168-018-0413-8
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: replicated Populus individuals
Observational units: tissue-level microbiome samples
Sampling strategy: Niche-level sampling across Populus tissues, broader plant habitats, soils, and host genotypes.
Replication: Replicated Populus individuals sampled across approximately 30 tissue-level niches.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on the primary publication and study description.

Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
plant habitat class
Broad Populus habitat class used to organize niche-level microbiome comparisons.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels leaf, stem, root, soil
Entity Populus-associated microbial habitat
Fields habitat, plant_habitat, sample_type
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
tissue-level niche
Fine-scale tissue or niche sampled within Populus plants and associated soils.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Levels approximately 30 tissue-level niches
Entity Populus tissue-level microbial habitat
Fields niche, tissue, compartment
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
Populus genotype
Host genotype used to test genotype effects on Populus-associated microbial communities.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Entity Populus host plant
Fields genotype, host_genotype
MIxS host genotype MIXS:0000365
bacterial archaeal and fungal community composition
16S and ITS profiles used to compare microbial communities across Populus niches and genotypes.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity Populus-associated microbial community
Matrix leaves, stems, roots, and soils
Method 16S rRNA and ITS amplicon sequencing

MEDIUM Cultivating Bacterial Microbiota of Populus Roots (Dale Pelletier)

Isolation and characterization of culturable bacteria from Populus roots with 16S sequencing for community analysis.

Organism: Populus trichocarpa
Data modalities:
Amplicon 16s Genomics
Samples: 552

Primary reference: doi:10.1128/mSystems.01306-20 - Cultivating the Bacterial Microbiota of <i>Populus</i> Roots

Experimental units: Populus root-associated bacterial cultures
Observational units: Populus root microbiome samples, bacterial isolate records
Sampling strategy: Isolation and 16S-based characterization of culturable bacteria from Populus roots.
Replication: 552 samples or isolate-related records.

Array

Matrix root

Fields asv_table, otu_table, community_profile
HIGH Climate-driven Divergence in Plant-Microbiome Interactions (Jason Holliday)

Range-wide survey examining how tree genotypes condition their soil microbiomes across the geographic distribution of Populus angustifolia. Demonstrates that warming-adapted microbiomes can transmit thermotolerance to axenic laboratory-grown plants. PMI Variovorax collection (n=190 strains) used for functional validation of microbially-mediated thermotolerance.

Organism: Populus angustifolia
Data modalities:
Amplicon 16s Amplicon Its
Samples: 539
BioProjects: PRJNA726831
Primary reference: doi:10.1038/s42003-021-02244-5
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: Populus angustifolia genotypes, microbiome inoculum sources
Observational units: soil microbiome samples, axenic plant thermotolerance assays
Sampling strategy: Range-wide sampling of Populus angustifolia soil microbiomes and functional validation of warming-adapted microbiomes.
Replication: 539 samples with functional validation using PMI Variovorax strains.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.
Study Variables 5
Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
climate or geographic origin
Geographic or climate origin of Populus angustifolia genotype or microbiome source.
Study Design Independent Variable Spatial Index Grouping
Categorical
Entity Populus-microbiome source environment
Fields geographic_origin, climate_origin, source_location
MIxS broad-scale environmental context MIXS:0000012
MIxS local environmental context MIXS:0000013
Populus angustifolia genotype
Host tree genotype used to evaluate conditioning of the soil microbiome.
Study Design Independent Variable Grouping
Categorical
Entity Populus angustifolia
Fields genotype, host_genotype, tree_genotype
MIxS host genotype MIXS:0000365
soil microbiome composition
Bacterial and fungal community composition associated with Populus genotypes across the species range.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity Populus-associated soil microbiome
Matrix soil
Method 16S and ITS amplicon sequencing
Fields bacterial_community_profile, fungal_community_profile, asv_table
MIxS environmental medium MIXS:0000014
microbially mediated thermotolerance
Thermotolerance phenotype transmitted to axenic plants by warming-adapted microbiomes or Variovorax validation strains.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity axenic Populus plant
Method plant thermotolerance assay
Fields thermotolerance, heat_tolerance, plant_growth_under_warming
Variovorax validation strain
Variovorax strain used for functional validation of microbially mediated thermotolerance.
Study Design Independent Variable Identifier
Identifier
Levels PMI Variovorax collection strains
Entity Variovorax strain
Fields variovorax_strain, strain_id, isolate_id
Populus Rhizosphere PD10 Synthetic Community (Mitchel J. Doktycz)

Ten-member synthetic bacterial community (PD10) assembled from Populus deltoides rhizosphere isolates to study assembly dynamics, medium-dependent community structure, and metabolic exchange during stable community formation.

Organism: Populus deltoides
Data modalities:
Genomics Metabolomics
Primary reference: doi:10.1128/mSystems.01306-20 - Cultivating the Bacterial Microbiota of <i>Populus</i> Roots
Study Design
Design type: time series design OBI:0500020
Experimental units: PD10 synthetic community cultures
Observational units: synthetic community abundance profiles, metabolite profiles
Sampling strategy: Time-resolved profiling of a ten-member Populus rhizosphere synthetic community across growth media and assembly stages.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description.

Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
PD10 member strain
Member strain in the ten-member Populus deltoides rhizosphere synthetic community.
Study Design Independent Variable Identifier Grouping
Identifier
Levels ten Populus deltoides rhizosphere isolates

Fields strain_id, member_id, syncom_member
growth medium

Categorical
Entity synthetic community culture
Fields medium, growth_medium, culture_condition
MIxS experimental factor MIXS:0000008
assembly time point
Time point during PD10 community assembly and stable community formation.

Categorical
Entity synthetic community sampling event
Time series yes
Fields time_point, assembly_time, sampling_time
member abundance profile
Relative or absolute abundance profile of PD10 member strains through community assembly.

Array
Entity PD10 synthetic community
Method sequencing-based community profiling
Fields member_abundance, strain_abundance, community_profile
metabolite exchange profile
Metabolite production, depletion, or exchange inferred during PD10 stable community formation.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable Derived
Array
Entity PD10 synthetic community metabolism
Matrix culture medium
Method metabolomics
Fields metabolite_abundance, metabolite_exchange, exometabolome_profile
Analyses & KBase Narratives 2
Populus Microbiome Narrative

Genome collection of Populus-associated microbes showing rhizosphere diversity. Includes isolate genomes and annotations from PMI bacterial collection.

Type: Kbase Narrative
Output types:
Genomics
PMI Synthetic Community Metabolic Modeling

CommScores analysis for predicting pairwise interactions among PMI bacterial isolates. Developed in collaboration with the PMI SFA.

Type: Kbase Narrative
Output types:
Genomics
Reference: doi:10.1016/j.csbj.2021.03.034
Key Publications 10
doi:10.1038/s41597-024-03069-7
  • Comprehensive multi-omics dataset of 318 metagenomes from 27 Populus genotypes
doi:10.1128/msystems.00886-23
  • Strong seasonal patterns drive Populus-associated microbial community structure
doi:10.1186/s40168-018-0413-8
  • Plant tissue niche has stronger effect on microbiome than host genotype
doi:10.1016/j.csbj.2021.03.034
  • Genome-scale metabolic modeling enables prediction of microbial interactions
doi:10.1093/hr/uhad085
  • Established CRISPR tools for Populus genetic engineering
doi:10.1038/s42003-021-02244-5 Climate-driven divergence in plant-microbiome interactions generates range-wide variation in bud break phenology
  • Warming-adapted microbiomes can transmit thermotolerance to axenic laboratory-grown plants
  • Tree genotypes condition their soil microbiomes across geographic distribution
doi:10.1128/mSystems.00045-18
  • Populus microbiome encodes diverse biosynthetic gene clusters for secondary metabolites
doi:10.1186/s12866-021-02370-4
  • Metaproteomics reveals dynamic regulation of microbial communities responding to environmental disturbance
doi:10.1094/MPMI-05-18-0133-R
  • Mortierella elongata PMI93 promotes P. trichocarpa growth in a cultivar-independent manner
doi:10.1186/s13007-023-01022-0 A rapid assay for assessing bacterial effects on Arabidopsis thermotolerance
  • Variovorax strains from Populus roots confer thermotolerance to plants
  • Six Variovorax strains (CF313, YR634, GV004, GV035, YR752, OV084) significantly improve plant heat tolerance
Datasets 3
PMI Bacterial Collection

Isolates from native, field, and greenhouse grown Populus, identified by 16S rDNA sequencing.

URL: https://pmiweb.ornl.gov/table-test/
Populus trichocarpa GWAS Population

>1500 natural variants of P. trichocarpa resequenced and phenotyped for genome-wide association studies (GWAS) linking plant genotype to microbiome composition. Includes backcross pedigrees for QTL mapping and transgenic lines in multiple host backgrounds.

Populus deltoides Common Garden Collection

~350 natural variants of P. deltoides planted in common gardens for studying genotype-environment interactions and microbiome assembly.

SEED SEED: Secure Ecosystem Engineering and Design

SEED develops predictive understanding of how microorganisms establish, spread, and influence ecosystems critical to DOE missions. Combines expertise in microbiology, plant biology, ecology, genetic engineering, and computational sciences. Currently deploying biodesign tools in the plant growth-promoting bacteria Bacillus velezensis to improve Populus resistance to the pathogenic fungus Sphaerulina musiva.

Lead institution: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Funding: DOE BER / Genomic Science Program
Principal investigators:
Paul Abraham
Laboratory Research Manager
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Xiaohan Yang
Lead Scientist
Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Links:
Main Ornl Page Doe Page Team
Scientific questions:
  • What factors influence microbial invasion success?
  • How do introduced microbes establish and spread in ecosystems?
  • How can we engineer ecosystems for DOE missions?
  • What risks do engineered organisms pose to ecosystems?
  • How can we detect and quantify microbial activity in situ?
Keywords:
Ecosystem Engineering Biosystems Design Microbial Invasion Biodefense Biosecurity Fungal Pathogens Crispr Rhizosphere
Data types:
Genomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics Imaging Data
Studies 3
LOW Bacillus velezensis EB14 Biocontrol Agent Genome (Paul Abraham)

Genome sequencing of Bacillus velezensis EB14, a native endophytic bacterial strain isolated from Populus × jackii with biocontrol potential against Sphaerulina musiva, the causative pathogen of poplar stem canker. Contains gene clusters for antimicrobial compound biosynthesis including iturin A, subtulene A, and fengycin.

Organism: Bacillus velezensis
Data modalities:
Genomics
BioProjects: PRJNA681597
Primary reference: doi:10.1094/PHYTO-09-20-0433-A
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Observational units: Bacillus velezensis EB14 genome assembly, antimicrobial biosynthetic gene clusters
Sampling strategy: Genome sequencing and annotation of a Populus endophytic bacterial strain with biocontrol potential.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.

Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
bacterial strain identifier
Identifier for the Bacillus velezensis EB14 biocontrol strain.
Identifier
Identifier
Levels EB14
Entity Bacillus velezensis strain
Fields strain_id, isolate_id, organism
host isolation source
Populus host source from which EB14 was isolated.
Grouping Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
Levels Populus x jackii
Entity bacterial isolation context
Matrix Populus endosphere
Fields host, isolation_source, host_common_name

antimicrobial biosynthetic gene cluster
Predicted biosynthetic gene cluster for antimicrobial compounds such as iturin A, subtulene A, or fengycin.
Derived Measured Variable
Array
Entity Bacillus velezensis genome
Method genome annotation and biosynthetic gene cluster prediction
Fields biosynthetic_gene_cluster, BGC, antimicrobial_cluster
antifungal biocontrol activity
Biocontrol or antifungal activity against Sphaerulina musiva.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity Bacillus velezensis EB14
Method antifungal activity assay
Fields antifungal_activity, inhibition_zone, biocontrol_activity
MEDIUM Populus-Laccaria Symbiosis Effector Discovery (Xiaohan Yang)

Investigation of plant-derived effector proteins mediating symbiosis between Populus and the ectomycorrhizal fungus Laccaria bicolor. Identifies PtLecRLK1 receptor and small secreted proteins regulating fungal colonization.

Organism: Populus trichocarpa
Data modalities:
Genomics Transcriptomics Metabolomics
Primary reference: doi:10.1093/hr/uhae232
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: Populus-Laccaria interaction assays
Observational units: fungal colonization measurements, effector and receptor expression profiles
Sampling strategy: Multi-omics investigation of Populus-derived effector proteins and Laccaria colonization during ectomycorrhizal symbiosis.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description and primary publication.

Variable Roles Value Context Mappings

Plant or fungal partner represented in the Populus-Laccaria symbiosis assay.

Categorical

Fields organism, partner, species

effector or receptor gene
Plant-derived effector protein, receptor, or small secreted protein implicated in symbiosis.
Study Design Independent Variable Identifier
Identifier
Levels PtLecRLK1, small secreted proteins
Entity symbiosis effector or receptor
Fields gene_id, effector_id, receptor_id
fungal colonization phenotype
Laccaria colonization response regulated by Populus effector and receptor interactions.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Numeric
Entity Populus-Laccaria symbiosis
Method fungal colonization assay
Fields colonization, fungal_colonization, symbiosis_phenotype
symbiosis omics profile
Transcriptomic or metabolomic profile used to discover symbiosis effectors.
Study Design Dependent Variable Measured Variable
Array
Entity Populus-Laccaria interaction
Method transcriptomics and metabolomics
Fields transcript_abundance, metabolite_abundance, omics_profile
LOW CRISPRi High-Throughput Screens in Non-Model Bacteria (Paul Abraham)

Development of workflow for rapid enhancement of transformation efficiency and genetic part characterization in non-model bacteria for genome-wide CRISPRi screens. Tools being deployed in Bacillus velezensis for rhizosphere studies.

Organism: Bacillus velezensis
Data modalities:
Genomics
Source: Synthetic Biology Tools to Reliably Establish and Monitor Microbial Invasions in the Rhizosphere
DOE Genomic Science abstract describing the SEED workflow for transformation optimization, genetic part characterization, and genome-wide CRISPRi screens in Bacillus velezensis.
Study Design
Design type: study design OBI:0500000
Experimental units: engineered non-model bacterial strains
Observational units: transformation efficiency measurements, CRISPRi guide-target records
Sampling strategy: High-throughput workflow development for transformation optimization, genetic part characterization, and genome-wide CRISPRi screens.
Notes: Variable catalog entry based on study description.

Variable Roles Value Context Mappings
non-model bacterial strain
Bacterial strain or chassis used for transformation optimization and CRISPRi screening.

transformation workflow condition
Protocol condition varied to enhance transformation efficiency.
Experimental Factor Study Design Independent Variable
Categorical
